navigation.groupScopeContent: Applies to {{scopeRoot}} only. Useful for group-specific timeouts and integrations.
navigation.globalScopeContent: Applies to your whole CCCC instance (daemon + Web). These settings affect all groups unless overridden.

actorProfiles.name: Profile Name
actorProfiles.commandOverrideRequired: Command override is enabled. Please enter a command.
actorProfiles.customRuntimeCommandRequired: Custom runtime with PTY requires a command.
actorProfiles.capabilityDefaultsHint: Set startup autoload capabilities for actors using this profile.
actorProfiles.envHint: Stored as profile secrets. Values are never shown in plain text.
actorProfiles.duplicateSecretsHint: Duplicating from "{{source}}": existing variable keys are shown here and will be copied on save.
actorProfiles.secretsHint: Write-only and stored locally under CCCC_HOME/state/secrets. Hover configured keys to preview masked values.
actorProfiles.deleteInUseConfirm: This profile is still linked by {{count}} actor(s).
actorProfiles.deleteInUseForce: Delete anyway and convert linked actors to custom?
actorProfiles.saveSecretsFailed: Profile saved, but failed to update secrets.

capabilities.title: Capability Governance
capabilities.subtitle: Global rules, sources, and registry for built-in and external capabilities.
capabilities.pageGuide: Global governance only. Configure external policy, manage sources, and inspect registry state. Runtime activation belongs to actor profiles and runtime tools.
capabilities.sourcesHint: Manage external capability feeds and inspect their sync health.
capabilities.libraryHint: Browse known capabilities, inspect readiness, and manage global block rules.
capabilities.requireGroup: Open this tab from a group to update block/unblock.
capabilities.blockedListHint: Capabilities blocked at the global level.
capabilities.failedSafetyMode: Failed to update external capability safety mode.
capabilities.safetyModeHint: Controls the default actionability of external capabilities. Built-in packs are unaffected.
capabilities.safetyModeCurrentRule: External sources currently follow the {{mode}} posture.

normal.label: Standard
normal.hint: External sources are usable by default, subject to per-source and per-capability overrides.

conservative.label: Conservative
conservative.hint: External sources default to a stricter indexed posture until explicitly relaxed.

readiness.blockedBySafetyMode: Blocked by conservative external import safety mode.

next.switch_external_capability_safety_mode_o: Relax safety mode or adjust allowlist

groupSpace.openFromGroup: Open this tab from a group.
groupSpace.title: Notebook Binding
groupSpace.description: Connect Google and choose the notebooks used for Work and Memory. Agents handle the rest.
groupSpace.loadFailed: Failed to load notebook binding state.
groupSpace.accountHint: Connect your Google account before selecting notebooks.
groupSpace.connectStartFailed: Failed to start Google connect flow.
groupSpace.connectCancelFailed: Failed to cancel Google connect flow.
groupSpace.workNotebookHint: Used for shared project knowledge and working materials.
groupSpace.noNotebookOptionsHint: No notebooks were found yet. You can create and bind a new one.
groupSpace.connectGoogleFirst: Connect Google first to manage notebook bindings.
groupSpace.workUnbindConfirm: Unbind the current Work Notebook? Agents will stop using it until another notebook is bound.
groupSpace.memoryUnbindConfirm: Unbind the current Memory Notebook? Agents will stop using it for memory recall until another notebook is bound.

automation.title: Automation
automation.description: Configure automation rules and policies. Settings are stored in
automation.openFromGroup: Open this tab from a group.
automation.rulesDescription: Define trigger conditions and actions (notify, group state, actor control).
automation.versionConflict: Automation rules were updated elsewhere. Latest version loaded; please reapply your edits.
automation.versionConflictShort: Automation rules were updated elsewhere. Latest version loaded.
automation.noCompletedToClear: No completed one-time reminders to clear.
automation.resetConfirm: Reset automation rules and message snippets to defaults? This replaces your current setup.
automation.snippetInvalid: Snippet name is invalid. Use letters/numbers/_/- (max 64 chars).
automation.editHint: Edit opens full settings in a modal to keep this list clean. Message snippets are managed in Snippets.
automation.draftHint: Changes apply to draft immediately. Click Save in Automation Rules to persist.
automation.resetTitle: Reset rules and snippets to defaults
automation.operationalActionsHint: Set Group Status and Control Actor Runtimes are available only for One-Time schedule.
automation.createSnippetFirst: Create at least one snippet before selecting Snippet.
automation.operationalActionsOnly: Operational actions are available only with One-Time schedule.
automation.noSnippetsYet: No snippets yet. Create one in Snippets first.
automation.messagePlaceholder: Write the message sent when this rule runs.
automation.resetGuidanceConfirm: Reset {{kind}}? This will delete {{filename}} override under CCCC_HOME. This cannot be undone.
automation.removeRegistryConfirm: Remove {{count}} missing group entries from registry?

policies.title: Engine Policies
policies.description: Built-in follow-ups and alerts. Adjust values, then click Save Policies.
policies.unreadFollowupHelp: Remind a member when unread messages sit too long.
policies.digestMinGapHelp: Minimum gap between digests for the same member.
policies.maxRepeatsHelp: Maximum follow-ups for one pending item.
policies.escalateAfterHelp: Escalate when repeat count reaches this value.
policies.keepaliveDelayHelp: Wait time after an actor says 'Next:'.
policies.actorIdleAlertHelp: Alert foreman if actor is inactive for this long.
policies.groupSilenceCheckHelp: Alert foreman if the entire group is silent.

ruleEditor.oneTimeOnly: This action supports one-time scheduling only.
ruleEditor.recurringHint: Recurring: run daily, weekly, or monthly.
ruleEditor.oneTimeHint: One-Time: run once by countdown or exact time.
ruleEditor.personalRule: Personal rule (owner: {{owner}}). Scope is controlled by permissions.

ruleList.noRules: No rules yet. Create one or reset to defaults.

snippetModal.title: Snippets
snippetModal.description: Reusable notification messages for automation rules.

delivery.title: Delivery
delivery.description: Control how messages and system notifications are injected into PTY runtimes.
delivery.throttleDescription: Tune pacing and read-cursor behavior. Set values to 0 to disable.
delivery.autoMarkReadHelp: Automatically advance read cursor after a message is successfully delivered to PTY.

messaging.title: Messaging
messaging.description: Configure how messages are routed when no explicit recipient is selected.
messaging.defaultRecipientDescription: Applies when <0>to</0> is empty (Web send with no recipients, IM <0>/send</0> with no <0>@targets</0>, MCP/CLI/SDK without <0>to</0>).
messaging.tip: Tip: Use explicit recipients for unambiguous routing.

imBridge.title: IM Bridge
imBridge.description: Connect this group to Telegram, Slack, Discord, Feishu/Lark, or DingTalk.
imBridge.botTokenHintSlack: Paste xoxb-… token or an env var name; required for outbound messages.
imBridge.botTokenHint: Paste the bot token or an env var name; required for bot authentication.
imBridge.appTokenHint: Optional; needed for inbound messages (Socket Mode).
imBridge.feishuRegionHint: Feishu and Lark share the same APIs but use different domains. Pick the one where your app was created.
imBridge.feishuPackageHint: Inbound streaming requires the Python package <0>lark-oapi</0> on the host running CCCC.
imBridge.appIdHint: App ID value or an env var name.
imBridge.appSecretHint: App Secret value or an env var name.
imBridge.appKeyHint: App Key value or an env var name.
imBridge.robotCodeHint: Optional; used when the session webhook is unavailable or expired.
imBridge.dingtalkPackageHint: Inbound streaming requires the Python package <0>dingtalk-stream</0> on the host running CCCC.
imBridge.requestHint: Step 1: In your IM chat, send /subscribe to request a temporary key. Step 2: the request will appear below in Pending Requests; click Approve (or paste the key in Bind). If foreman is online, you can forward the key and ask foreman to bind it for you.
imBridge.requestCopied: Copied /subscribe. Send it in your IM chat to request a key, then approve it from Pending Requests (or bind by key).
imBridge.bindError: Key does not exist or has expired.
imBridge.verboseOnHint: Receiving all messages. Click to receive user-only messages.
imBridge.verboseOffHint: Receiving user-only messages. Click to receive all messages.
imBridge.setupStep1: Create a bot on your IM platform
imBridge.setupStep2: Set the token(s) as environment variable(s)
imBridge.setupStep3: Save the config and start the bridge
imBridge.setupStep4: In your IM chat, send /subscribe to receive messages

transcript.title: Terminal transcript
transcript.description: Readable tail for troubleshooting. User can always view; agent access is controlled by the policy below.
transcript.visibilityTip: Tip: This only affects agents. The Web UI user can always view transcripts.
transcript.tailViewerHint: Best-effort transcript tail from the actor PTY ring buffer.

guidance.openFromGroup: Open this tab from a group.
guidance.preambleHint: Injected automatically before the first delivery after start/restart.
guidance.resetHint: Delete override file and fall back to built-in

blueprint.openFromGroup: Open this tab from a group.
blueprint.exportDescription: Save this group's actors, settings, automation rules, and guidance as a single portable file.
blueprint.importDescription: Applies a blueprint by replacing actors, settings, automation rules/snippets, and group guidance overrides (CCCC_HOME). Ledger history is never modified.
blueprint.pickFileFirst: Pick a file to preview first
blueprint.replaceConfirm:
Replace this group's actors, settings, automation rules, and guidance using "{{filename}}"?

This will stop running agents and then apply group guidance overrides under CCCC_HOME:
- CCCC_PREAMBLE.md
- CCCC_HELP.md

developer.title: Developer Mode (Global)
developer.description: These settings apply to the whole CCCC instance (daemon + Web). Use this only when debugging.
developer.warningText: Developer mode enables verbose logs and extra diagnostics. Only enable it when you need it.
developer.enableHint: Enables debug snapshot and log tail tools.
developer.terminalBuffersHint: Controls how much terminal history is kept. Larger buffers use more memory (scales with running actors and opened terminals).
developer.ptyBacklogHint: Affects initial history and transcript tail. Takes full effect after restarting an actor.
developer.webScrollbackHint: Controls how far you can scroll up in the Web terminal.
developer.registryDescription: Scan missing/corrupt groups in registry. Cleanup only removes missing entries.
developer.debugSnapshotHint: Shows daemon/actors state + delivery throttle summary (developer mode only).
developer.openFromGroup: Open Settings from an active group to view group-scoped debug info.
developer.logTailHint: Tails local log files (developer mode only). Component "im" is group-scoped.
developer.imLogsRequireGroup: IM logs require a group_id; open Settings from a group.

webAccess.title: Web Access
webAccess.description: Manage Web reachability, Access Tokens, and interaction mode in one place.
webAccess.loadFailed: Failed to load Web Access state.
webAccess.saveFailed: Failed to save Web Access settings.

summary.localOnlyHint: The Web console is only reachable from this machine.
summary.remoteEnabledHint: Remote access is available and ready to use.
summary.remoteNeedsAttentionHint: Remote access is configured but still blocked by a prerequisite or binding issue.
summary.openHint: No Access Tokens exist yet. Any browser on this machine can use Web until you create one.
summary.protectedHint: {{count}} Access Token configured. Browsers without a token must sign in, even on localhost.
summary.exhibitHint: The Web UI is running in read-only exhibit mode.

webAccess.accessControlDescription: Create admin or scoped Access Tokens. Once at least one token exists, browsers without a token must sign in to use Web APIs, even on localhost.
webAccess.firstTokenAdminHint: The first access token must be admin so you do not lock yourself out.
webAccess.adminTokenHint: Admin access tokens can manage global settings and other access tokens.
webAccess.allGroupsHint: Leave scope open unless you specifically want to restrict this token.
webAccess.customTokenPlaceholder: Leave empty to generate a secure random token
webAccess.newTokenHint: Copy and store this token now. The full value is not shown again in normal lists.
webAccess.noAccessTokens: No access tokens yet. The Web console is currently open by default on this machine.
webAccess.deleteConfirm: Delete this access token? This cannot be undone.
webAccess.reachabilityDescription: Configure how the Web console is exposed beyond localhost, and whether remote exposure requires access tokens.
webAccess.requireAccessTokenHint: This only affects remote exposure. Local Web login still becomes active as soon as any Access Token exists.
webAccess.noEndpoint: No remote endpoint is currently available.
webAccess.noNextSteps: No additional action is suggested right now.
webAccess.interactionModeDescription: Exhibit is a runtime launch mode, not another auth mechanism.
webAccess.modeGuideNormal: Normal mode is the standard interactive control plane.
webAccess.modeGuideExhibit: Exhibit makes the Web UI read-only for public or spectator viewing.
webAccess.modeGuideSwitch: To switch modes, restart Web with or without the exhibit flag.
webAccess.howItWorksActivateLogin: As soon as any Access Token exists, Web login becomes active for every browser session, including localhost.
webAccess.howItWorksAutoLogin: The browser that creates a new Access Token is signed in automatically with that token.
webAccess.howItWorksRemotePolicy: The remote exposure toggle below only controls whether remote access may run without tokens. It does not disable local Web login.
webAccess.newTokenAutoLoginHint: This browser is already signed in with the new token.
webAccess.newTokenVerifyHint: Use an incognito window or another browser if you want to verify the login screen.
webAccess.currentBrowserOpenHint: No Access Tokens exist yet, so this browser does not need to sign in.
webAccess.currentBrowserSignedInHint: This browser is signed in as {{userId}} ({{role}}).
webAccess.currentBrowserNotSignedInHint: Login is active, but this browser is not signed in yet.
